title: Ammonia and ammonia/hydrogen combustion: Comprehensive quantitative assessment of kinetic models and examination of critical parameters
notes: Evaluates 16 models; direct precedent that rules out a simple mechanism-ranking paper.

title: Experimental and kinetic modeling study of laminar burning velocities of NH3/air, NH3/H2/air, NH3/CO/air and NH3/CH4/air premixed flames
notes: NH3/H2 subset is relevant; complete campaign must remain grouped.

title: Experimental and numerical analysis of the autoignition behavior of NH3 and NH3/H2 mixtures at high pressure
notes: 20-75 bar and 1040-1210 K; simulation must use reported volume histories when available.

title: Study of the oxidation of ammonia in a flow reactor. Experiments and kinetic modeling simulation
notes: Pure NH3 contextual/species validation; relevance to cracked fuel must be bounded.

title: Experimental and numerical study of the laminar burning velocity of NH3/H2/air premixed flames at elevated pressure and temperature
notes: 298-493 K and 0.1-0.7 MPa; stretch correction must be audited.

title: Low- and intermediate-temperature ammonia/hydrogen oxidation in a flow reactor: Experiments and a wide-range kinetic modeling
notes: High-priority source because data and CHEMKIN mechanism are reported as supplementary material.

title: An experimental and kinetic modelling study on the oxidation of NH3, NH3/H2, NH3/CH4 in a variable pressure laminar flow reactor at engine-relevant conditions
notes: NH3/H2 subset and elevated-pressure speciation are relevant.

title: Experimental and chemical kinetic study for the combustion of ammonia-hydrogen mixtures
notes: Potential multi-observable campaign; development data cannot be external validation for its own mechanism.

title: A study on measuring ammonia-hydrogen IDTs and constructing an ammonia-hydrogen combustion mechanism at engine-relevant thermodynamic and fuel concentration conditions
notes: Exact volume history and ignition definition required.

title: Experimental investigation and optimization of ammonia-hydrogen chemical kinetics with ignition delay times from shock tubes
notes: 373 IDTs targeted in optimization; DLR and UCF campaigns must be separate groups.

title: Rush-to-equilibrium concept for minimizing reactive nitrogen emissions in ammonia combustion
notes: Strong overlap with finite-residence reactive-nitrogen framing.

title: A detailed kinetic mechanism for ammonia combustion informed by DFT calculations
notes: Recent mechanism candidate; exact downloadable version required.

title: A novel data consistency analysis for experimental laminar burning velocities of NH3-H2 mixtures
notes: Requires explicit handling of cross-laboratory inconsistency and reported uncertainties.

title: Measurement of laminar burning velocity and OH/NO concentrations in preheated partially cracked ammonia and kinetic analysis
notes: Direct partially cracked NH3 evidence; likely strongest LBV/NO external-validation candidate.

title: Temperature dependence of the laminar burning velocity for partially cracked ammonia/air flames
notes: Evaluates 17 mechanisms and documents inter-study inconsistency; close novelty competitor.

title: Comparative analysis of combustion and emissions performance of partially cracked ammonia and NH3/H2 flames in a model gas turbine combustor
notes: Direct operating/emissions overlap; ensemble external validation must be the differentiator.

title: Investigating the emissions and flame structures of lean premixed partially cracked ammonia flames stabilized in a bluff-body burner
notes: Direct emissions and flame-structure evidence; use only conditions compatible with canonical models.

title: Flame structure and emissions in two-stage combustion with partially cracked ammonia
notes: Complex flow limits direct 0D/1D validation but constrains engineering interpretation.

title: Laminar burning characteristics of ammonia and hydrogen blends at elevated initial pressures up to 2.5 MPa
notes: Publisher PDF archived with SHA256 5F28FB444F84C92D2BA08008A77BBCEFE7E28D28025594E890887E914286DD89. Reported range: 0.1-2.5 MPa, phi 0.6-1.4 and H2 volume fraction 0.1-0.6. No numerical values have been inferred from figures.

title: Experimental and kinetic modeling investigation on the laminar flame propagation of ammonia under oxygen enrichment and elevated pressure conditions
notes: Official 38-species YAML archived and validated; complete study excluded from the primary external LBV gate to prevent self-validation leakage.

title: A new detailed kinetic model for surrogate fuels: C3MechV3.3
notes: C3-v3.5_C0 is an official ammonia-only extraction related to C3-v3.5; the institutional record cites the C3MechV3.3 paper and identifies the C3 consortium.

title: Experimental and kinetic modeling investigation on the laminar flame propagation of ammonia/hydrogen/air mixtures at elevated pressures
notes: Official supplement contains experiment workbook, mechanism, thermo and transport. Complete study excluded from the primary external LBV gate.

title: Chemical kinetic modeling of ammonia oxidation with improved reaction mechanism for ammonia/air and ammonia/hydrogen/air combustion
notes: Mechanism, thermo and transport were retained from one official ScienceDirect release.

title: The combustion chemistry of ammonia and ammonia/hydrogen mixtures: A comprehensive chemical kinetic modeling study
notes: Official mechanism, thermo and transport archived; distinct from NUIGMech1.1-HT.

title: Combustion chemistry of ammonia/C1 fuels: A comprehensive kinetic modeling study
notes: Complete C1 model archived. First staged LBV case exceeded 900.3 s and about 0.58 GB, so 1D exclusion is computational rather than predictive.

title: Detailed kinetic model for combustion of NH3/H2 blends
notes: Burke-linear variant is primary; standard variant is retained only as a same-study ablation. Derived species-name normalization is fully logged.
